submitted_payload.suggested_entry: - Ralph Workflow — free and open-source workflow layer for unattended coding runs that aims to hand back finished, tested code ready to review instead of another confident summary

why_this_action: Shared artifacts still show distribution_and_message_to_primary_repo_conversion as the live bottleneck, Codeberg adoption is flat, GitHub auth is unavailable for direct PR submission, and Kingy AI was a prepared but unsent comparison-adjacent editorial target with a legitimate public email path.
shared_findings_used: adoption_metrics_latest.md: Codeberg remains flat and is still the primary success gate
curator_outreach_queue_latest.json: Kingy AI was prepared but unsent before this send
market_intelligence_latest.json: reuse canonical positioning and competitor adjacency instead of inventing a new pitch
seo-reports/comparisons/conductor-oss.md and hermes-agent.md: keep the message anchored on workflow and reviewability, not generic orchestration hype

measurement_window.success_signal: reply, mention, listing inclusion, or a new qualified backlink path that sends evaluators to the Codeberg primary repo

repairs_applied.change: Updated Kingy AI from prepared to sent_via_email_fallback so the queue reflects real execution and future runs do not duplicate this outreach.
Appended the outreach record so future distribution runs can dedupe against this live contact.
Removed Kingy AI from the top prepared handoff list and promoted TIMEWELL so the live packet stays truthful.
Updated the top waiting curator targets so the board no longer advertises an already-sent target as still waiting.
